description: Command body uses Bash (shell code blocks) but does not declare allowed-tools in frontmatter; least-privilege toolset undeclared
Command body uses Bash (shell code blocks) but does not declare allowed-tools in frontmatter; least-privilege toolset undeclared
Vague quantifier 'some' in user-facing output string: 'Context is good but has some bloat' — unmeasured amount
suggested_fix: Replace with measurable criterion, e.g. 'Context is good but overhead is 20-40% of window'

description: Vague quantifier 'Some' in behavioral instruction: 'Some optimizations have side effects' — does not specify which
suggested_fix: Enumerate which specific optimizations have side effects, or link to the detailed list that follows

description: Vague quantifier 'appropriate' in Core Rules: 'Use appropriate models (with fallbacks)' — the Model Selection table already specifies exact mappings; the prose rule adds no information
suggested_fix: Delete the prose rule or replace with 'Use the model tiers in the Model Selection table above'

description: subprocess.run(['start', str(path)], shell=True) on Windows branch of _open_in_browser(); pre-scan flagged as HIGH
fp_reason: 'start' is a Windows cmd.exe built-in that requires shell=True to invoke; argument is FLEET_DASHBOARD_PATH constructed from a hardcoded internal path with no user input
rule_gap: Rule should exempt platform-specific browser-open idioms (open/xdg-open/start) where the argument is a hardcoded or internally computed file path

description: subprocess.run(['powershell', '-Command', ps_cmd]) where ps_cmd is a dynamically formatted string; pre-scan flagged as HIGH
fp_reason: All dynamic values in ps_cmd are cast through int(pid) before interpolation, which prevents shell metacharacter injection; this is a standard Windows process-introspection pattern
rule_gap: Rule should account for int()-cast sanitization on numeric-only parameters passed into subprocess list-form commands

description: Generated fleet dashboard HTML hard-embeds Google Fonts CDN links (fonts.googleapis.com, fonts.gstatic.com); browser makes third-party network requests whenever user views the dashboard, leaking activity metadata to Google
suggested_fix: Bundle JetBrains Mono and Space Grotesk woff2 files locally under skills/fleet-auditor/assets/fonts/ and serve them as file:// references, or replace with a system font stack

description: Hooks registered across 10+ distinct event types (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCompact, PostCompact, SessionStart, Stop, SessionEnd, StopFailure, UserPromptSubmit, CwdChanged); any defect in hook scripts will execute on a very high proportion of user interactions
suggested_fix: Document the broad execution surface in README/PRIVACY.md; consider consolidating the three overlapping PreCompact hooks into one script with branching logic to reduce the registered count
